---
title: "About me"
description: |
  Hi there! I'm Alex.
  
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

I'm a neuroscientist currently living in Denver, Colorado, and relocating to the DC area soon to pursue postdoctoral training at the NIH. I grew up in Corvallis, Oregon, and Boise, Idaho, and came to Denver to pursue a PhD in Neuroscience at the University of Colorado, Anschutz Medical Campus. My research is described on my [Research](research.html) page. My career goal is to become an independent investigator in neuroscience and to teach statistics courses for researchers in the biological sciences.

During my undergraduate education I studied math and biology and worked full-time tutoring, TAing, and teaching math courses for 3 years. I'm passionate about math education and dismantling the idea that math isn't for everyone. I realized toward the end of my PhD that I have an opportunity to support good quantitative and statistical practices in biological research, [a largely unmet need](https://doi.org/10.1038/nn.2886), by teaching. So, in addition to being a postdoc in Neuroscience, I'm slowly chipping away at an MS in Statistics so that I can formally make math and stats education for biologists part of my academic career. 

Outside of the lab and classroom, I'm an avid [distance runner](misc.html), [cat mom](cauchy.html), [amateur artist](page.html), and board game enthusiast. I built this website with the R package {Distill}, which you can learn more about [here](https://rstudio.github.io/distill/).
